last_next_action.reason: execution queue is empty; wake the agent to synthesize or update the research agenda and advance all currently runnable safe project work from objective, evidence, human advice, and safety policy

recent_evidence.summary: Bridge-free weak-signal follow-up condition review after terminal det=1.25 Ramsey. The physical signature is a weak det-shift-consistent high-sideband candidate, but Ramsey remains ambiguous because target amplitudes are only about 0.5-1.0% and low-sideband/direct-Larmor/carrier support is incomplete. A non-blind follow-up candidate is auto__cpmg / CPMG.xml with N=8 and tau 0.45..1.60 us to cover both 1/(4 f13)=0.650 us and 1/(2 f13)=1.300 us conventions for f13=384.6 kHz. No bridge job was submitted; next bridge-touching work needs fresh intent, verifier, MATLAB advisory, queue-idle check, and current drift/window review.

c02 terminal det=1.25 Ramsey review and weak-13C follow-up condition review: Terminal det=1.25 MHz Ramsey for reimage1804_c02 completed cleanly at 06:41 EDT with healthy counts (44.073 kcps pre-run, 42.669 kcps post-run), 16 x 50000 reps over tau 0..10 us, snake scan order, and no drift flags. Readout2/self-baseline FFT target bins were direct 13C 0.54%, det-13C 0.79%, carrier 1.00%, det+13C 1.01%, old det=1 high/static 0.39%, static low 0.46%, previous 1.9 MHz 0.23%. Cross-det comparison keeps a weak det-shift-consistent high-sideband candidate (1.95/0.84/1.01% for det=1.5/1.0/1.25), but it is not a claim-grade Ramsey-only 13C observation. T2star is supported as short/few-us order, not a precise scalar. A bridge-free follow-up review identified a bounded CPMG tau-scan candidate but did not submit bridge work.

Authored an explicit CPMG N=8 weak-13C discriminator model/intent for reimage1804_c02 and materialized it after verifier/advisory/queue gates. Intent verifier JSON verdict was verified with no hard errors/blockers/warnings; queue was idle before enqueue. Single-submit advisory ok=true with no blockers, 37 tau points, 12 x 50000 reps, estimated 536.2 s per average under the active 900 s nighttime cap. Bridge job nv23_cpmg_20260515_072306_auto_cpmg is running at average 1/12 with Final about 42.714 kcps, monitor active/no last_error, stop_requested=false, queued/staging empty. This touched the bridge queue; do not submit/mutate further bridge jobs until terminal/anomaly evidence.

Bridge-free one-average autosave sanity review of running CPMG N=8 job nv23_cpmg_20260515_072306_auto_cpmg. Raw export verified CPMG.xml, tau 0.45..1.60 us, 37 points, one saved average x 50000 reps, snake scan order, and do_adiabatic_inversion=0. The bridge remained running with Final about 44.318 kcps, monitor active/no last_error, stop_requested=false. Readout count/roles match XML expectation: readout1 true-0 reference, readout2 pi/ms=1 reference, readout3 final CPMG echo candidate signal. Readout3/self-baseline at nearest target taus was 0.9995 near 0.642 us and 0.9839 near 1.312 us, i.e. no obvious target-tau dip in this single 50k-shot average. Readout2 reference had a high point near 1.312 us, so terminal review must compare raw/reference/self-baseline views. No 13C claim or rejection is made from this nonterminal one-average subset.

last_human_request.preview: Objective: Find a magnetic-field-aligned NV from image172647, then obtain a well-supported T2star and 13C conclusion. Start a project using the spatial range of <MATLAB_23C_ROOT>\SavedImages\3DXYZ-Image-2026-05-14-172647.mat as the initial search region. Identify an NV aligned to the magnetic field within that image range; if none is found, search different regions. Judge magnetic-field alignment using a strong-pi pulsed ODMR scan; if it does not show a clear usable resonance, reject
